free_text: Low-energy cross sections for B-11(p,3alpha)*
Proton beam.
B-11 beam.
Correction according to new rules. Angular
distributions and elastic scattering were added.
Last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. Several
Subents were merged. Subent 011 added.
All targets were fabricated by evaporation of natural
B-11 (81.5% B-11) onto 0.1 mm Ta backings. The targets
ranged in thickness from 2 to 100 ug/cm2.
Silicon surface-barrier detectors (with an
active area of 300 mm2) were positioned at 90. degr.
and 135. degr. to the beam direction in close geometry.
The alpha-particles were observed with Si detectors
positioned at 40., 60., 90., 120., 140., and 160. degr.
(placed at a distance of 6 to 16 cm from the target
and collimated by 2 to 3 mm diameter apertures in
front of the detectors).
Cross sections data were calculated by
compiler using formulae at page 342.
The errors includes all experimental
uncertainties excluding error due to normalization.
Error due to the cross section normaliation.
-By authors. Due to the thick targets used, data
obtained near 150-keV resonance do not represent cross
section values and have therefore been omitted.
Tbl.2
Data measured via 1H(11B,alpha)8Be
with a gas target
SD: ERR-T -> ERR-1. ERR-ANALYS, STATUS
updated. Data from Subent 003 were added. Data from
REACTION (S) code were moved in col. MISC (data were
not experimental). E-LVL -> LVL-NUMB (no information
in the text about energy level).
The B-11 heavy ion beam was bombarded onto a hydrogen
gas target of the quasi-point supersonic jet system
type.
The absolute cross section was determined at low
energies relative to the 11B+p elastic scattering cross
section
